text: Text similarity measurement is one of the common tasks in Natural Language Processing (NLP). It is applied in many applications including search engines, text classification, machine translation and automatic essay grading. This survey reviews the text similarity measurements techniques. It mainly focuses on three measurement approaches: string-based, corpus-based and knowledge-based measurements. In the string-based measurements, the techniques used are the matching at the character level and word level. While in the corpus-based measurements, it makes use of the statistical information present in the corpora to evaluate the degree of semantic relatedness between texts. And in the knowledge-based measurements, it refers to using the semantic relationship present in a knowledge base such as WordNet. The survey also reviews the hybrid models that combine various measurements to enhance the measurements performance. It aims at providing a technical reference for the researches and applications in the field of semantic and lexical analysis of texts.
explanation: The text uses generic survey phrasing and lacks specific details or novelty, a pattern often seen in AI-generated or template-based writing.
